new_classes.label_en: Blended Scotch whisky in containers > 2 L (subheading 2208 30 79)
new_classes.label_de: Blended Scotch Whisky in Behältern > 2 L (Unterposition 2208 30 79)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended Scotch whisky — a blend of one or more single malt Scotch whiskies with one or more single grain Scotch whiskies, distilled and matured in Scotland from a mash of cereals, marketed at an alcoholic strength by volume of 40% vol or higher, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres. Whisky-soda (whisky with added aerated water) is excluded.

coverage_explanation:
The proposed axioms capture the key discriminating criteria for CN code 2208 30 79 — Blended Scotch whisky in containers > 2 L:
1. ✅ Scotch whisky origin (distilled and matured in Scotland) — via ScotchWhiskyOriginQuality.
2. ✅ Blended Scotch composition (blend of one or more single malt Scotch whiskies with one or more single grain Scotch whiskies) — via BlendedScotchCompositionQuality and BlendedScotchWhiskyBlendingProcess.
3. ✅ Alcoholic strength ≥ 40% vol — via decimalRange restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent.
4. ✅ Container size > 2 L — via someValuesFrom LargeContainer (≥ 2 L; exact > 2 L cannot be distinguished from LargeContainer ≥ 2 L in the current TBox, which is a minor gap).
5. ✅ Exclusion of whisky-soda (whisky with added aerated water) — via complement restriction on SodaWaterAddedQuality.
6. ✅ Distinguishing from sibling BlendedScotchWhisky22083071 (containers ≤ 2 L) by the LargeContainer restriction.
7. ✅ Distinguishing from BlendedMalt subheadings via complement on BlendedMaltCompositionQuality.

Minor gap: The LargeContainer class in the TBox is defined as ≥ 2 L (minInclusive 2.0), while this subheading requires strictly > 2 L. The current TBox does not provide a strictly-greater-than-2L container class, so this boundary is slightly imprecise. The legal text note also references the broader whisky definition (distilled from cereal mash, marketed at ≥ 40% vol), which is fully captured. Coverage is therefore ~0.88 due to the ≥ 2 L vs. > 2 L container boundary imprecision.
